prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='CWT_catboost', seasonality_champion='THETA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.862) and very high seasonality variance (1.0085), with trend champions like CWT_catboost having low slope error while seasonality champions like THETA capture cyclic patterns best. Early horizon specialists (THETA, ETS) differ from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost), indicating horizon-specific performance. The data strongly supports DBA combination to handle the high disagreement and leverage different model strengths across horizons.

raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.25}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. High rmse_spread_ratio=0.862 and seasonality_corr_variance=1.0085 favor DBA combination for robust aggregation. With only n_windows=3, moderate shrinkage (0.25) prevents overfitting. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination as primary method to handle phase shifts and leverage trend/seasonality champions across horizons."
}
